title: Don't update health check status during initialDelaySeconds
body:
When InitialDelaySeconds in the kube yaml is set for a helthcheck,
don't update the healthcheck status till those initial delay seconds are over.
We were waiting to update for a failing healtcheck, but when the healthcheck
was successful during the initial delay time, the status was being updated as healthy
immediately.
This is misleading to the users wondering why their healthcheck takes
much longer to fail for a failing case while it is quick to succeed for
a healthy case. It also doesn't match what the k8s InitialDelaySeconds
does. This change is only for kube play, podman healthcheck run is
unaffected.

Fixes https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHELPLAN-161024.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
For kube play, the health check status will wait the full start period time before updating for a healthy state as it was already doing for an unhealthy state. This matches what k8s does with the InitialDelaySeconds.
```

comments.body: Cockpit tests failed for commit 47580eb42822bffd0d5e5f7cd64446290cd73b3b. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 7ba46e84d76ca80776e0d4404316023f6d984df2. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

K8s waits the `initialDelaySeconds` before running the probe command, while podman seems to run it from the get go and just waits to change the status if there is a wait period and the health check is failing. I guess it makes sense to report healthy as soon as the health checks pass for the service manager use cases and not have to wait the whole initial delay seconds - also looks like that is what docker does.

I will add a new option for the kube play use case and update the PR.

@umohnani8 note that this issue was also discussed/reported in #19272 and likely fixed it (.. eventually).

[That failing cockpit test](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/3033e64a-f7ce-4bf4-9bed-794965f4e46a/) creates a health check with a start period of 5s and an interval of 325s (read it as "infinity"). It expects that a "healthy" event gets sent after startup within 15s, so it should be compatible with both the current main behaviour of sending out the first event immediately (which is what #19272 is all about) and your fix which should delay the event by the start-period. However, this times out now, and the [screenshot](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/3033e64a-f7ce-4bf4-9bed-794965f4e46a/work-podman-systemuufg12g5/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-system/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/system-1/data/TestApplication-testHealthcheckSystem-fedora-39-127.0.0.1-22-FAIL.png) shows ("Checking health") that it didn't receive an event through the API yet.

Is that intended, perhaps in the sense of "need at least two passing health checks", or a bug?

Thanks!

~Need the c/image changes to be merged in first https://github.com/containers/image/pull/2201~

Update: Doesn't require any changes in c/image anymore.
